figures.caption: Table 12: Full variance decomposition. R2_cov is the fraction of CO variance explained by the binary has_sources flag (retrieved something vs. nothing; n ≈ 1,000). R2_qual is the fraction of CO variance explained by the continuous retrieval_quality score, restricted to answered queries (nans shown as range). Declining R2_cov for HyDE reflects reduced variance in the binary flag as coverage approaches 1.
Figure 3: Cost vs. Quality on real user queries.
Figure 1: The Coverage Illusion, showing retrieval coverage by workflow and query condition.
Figure 4: Experimental architecture: oracle benchmark construction (top), pre-retrieval routing baselines (a), and post-retrieval cascade (b).
Figure 5: Retrieval workflow tiers: Tier 1 (retrieval-only) and Tier 2 (LLM-augmented).
Table 16: R2 sensitivity analysis (real user queries). R2 remains stable as the coverage threshold tightens.

Table 14: Coverage-fixed quality for queries answered by all five workflows simultaneously (n varies by condition). Coverage is held at 100% across all workflows, so any differences reflect genuine per-answer quality.
